text: Paper First Title Reform of Polish water law – goals of the new law on water Author Ewelina BRYLA Magdalena LEJA Citation Acta Geophysica 61 (1) Pages 55-77 Published OnlineFirst: 16 September 2014 Abstract In this paper we present the reform of Polish water law which has been ongoing in recent years, focusing on the main goals of the proposed new law – Water Law Act. The subject matter of our analysis are, in particular, the fragmentation of managerial competences at various levels of governance, the clear separation of infrastructure management from water resource management, and a catchment-based policy with simpler methods of financial coverage of water investments. We present, based on our own comparative analysis of the existing law and new regulations, the factors which are responsible for the present deficiencies of water law and we discuss whether the law as proposed will indeed constitute an effective method of improving the performance of the national water management system.
